summary: Per-button auto-cancel, update overlay fix, L2 reliability, trading dock restore, sign-out popover fix.
release_notes: Auto-cancel orders on close is now a per-button toggle — set it independently for Close 50% and Close 100% under each bias tab in Settings → Trading.
Removed the single global auto-cancel toggle from the General section — the setting now lives next to each close button's config (both in Settings and the quick-settings gear popups).
Existing users' global auto-cancel preference is automatically migrated to all four close buttons on first load.
Closing a docked trading terminal now remembers the dock state — reopening restores it docked to the same chart slot.
Fixed login overlay flashing briefly when navigating back to the screener — the overlay is now hidden immediately if a session token exists.
Fixed sign-out popover hidden behind the trading panel — now renders above all other elements.
Removed stray yellow dot row from the Badges table on the Help page.
Updated the Auto-Update Help section to reflect that updates are forced on startup, not optional.
Update overlay redesigned — full-screen centered overlay (matching the disconnected overlay style) replaces the old green top banner.
Fixed auto-update download never showing progress — now displays downloaded MB even when the server doesn't send a Content-Length header.
Fixed auto-update errors being silently swallowed — download failures now set an error state and display the reason in the overlay.
Fixed macOS auto-update failing to extract the zip — pre-signed S3 URLs with query parameters are now parsed correctly.
Update overlay auto-dismisses on error or timeout instead of hanging on 'Downloading' forever.
Added 5-minute hard timeout on the update download to prevent indefinite hangs.
Fixed Level 2 depth freezing after some time — server now detects stale TWS depth subscriptions (no update in 30s) and automatically re-subscribes.
Fixed duplicate depth subscriptions leaking IB API slots — concurrent subscribe calls for the same ticker are now serialized with an in-flight guard.
Fixed race condition where unsubscribing could cancel a subscription that a new client still needs — unsub now re-checks for active listeners before cancelling.
L2 depth errors (max subscriptions, not available) now auto-retry up to 3 times instead of permanently killing the book.
Re-clicking the same ticker now forces a fresh L2 subscription if depth data stopped flowing.
Depth debounce timers and staleness trackers are properly cleared on TWS disconnect.
Login overlay is now guaranteed invisible (0ms) during valid sessions — inline CSS hides it before the DOM even paints.
Quick-settings gear popup dropdown values now display in BlitzTrade blue instead of grey.
Docked trading terminal titlebar no longer shows the move cursor — only undocked mode indicates draggability.
Trade button moved next to Help in the top-bar controls row with matching height.
Chart right-click Trade option is now hidden for scanner-only subscribers.
Added Trade option to the scanner table right-click menu (only visible with a trading subscription).
Scanner right-click Trade now behaves identically to the chart Trade option — preserves dock state and switches ticker in-place.
Fixed OTP login failing with 'Network error' — server-side _check_subscription was never defined, causing a NameError on verify and refresh.
Removed dev-mode auth bypass — login is now always required, even when Cognito env vars are not set.
serve.py auto-fetches Cognito IDs from CloudFormation when not set (dev mode with AWS CLI).
Added --cognito-pool and --cognito-client CLI args to serve.py, start.bat, and start.sh for dev without AWS CLI.
Fixed _check_subscription using wrong Cognito group names — now matches actual groups (scanner, scanner-trading, scanner-trading-analytics).
Fixed token refresh 403 (no subscription) silently failing — now forces logout with error message.
Toasts now visible on the login screen — moved toast container outside the app div so force-logout messages aren't hidden.
Account balance now displays in the account's native currency (€, £, etc.) instead of always showing $.
Fixed 403 on token refresh silently failing — now forces logout with error toast when subscription is removed.
